india_trends.title: Aurobindo Pharma arm CuraTeQ gets Health Canada nod for biosimilar
india_trends.summary: Aurobindo Pharma announced a significant development today. Its subsidiary, CuraTeQ Biologics, has received a compliance notice from Health Canada for its cancer drug Bevqolva. This approval marks a crucial step for the bevacizumab biosimilar. Bevqolva is used to treat various cancers. Health Canada

india_trends.title: Vedanta Aluminium told to pay Rs 233 crore for unauthorised water extraction in Odisha
india_trends.summary: Vedanta Aluminium faces a hefty Rs 233.11 crore demand for unauthorized water extraction. The company allegedly drew large volumes of water daily from Bheden river without permission. This includes base charges, a six-time penalty, and monthly interest. The Burla irrigation division has issued a one

india_trends.title: GIFT City treasury hub picks up pace; 6 listed companies seek licences
india_trends.summary: GIFT City is gaining traction as a destination for multinational companies looking to streamline their treasury operations. Recent applications for corporate treasury centre licenses highlight a growing interest in this financial hub, offering regulatory incentives and a robust framework.

india_trends.title: Coal India's production in April drops amid rising power demand
india_trends.summary: April brought unexpected challenges for Coal India, the giant behind India's coal production. The company experienced a significant 9.7 percent dip in output, stirring alarms as coal is the backbone of over 70 percent of the nation's electricity supply. With summer demand hitting unprecedented level

india_trends.title: India's sugar exports to be 7.5-8 lakh tonnes in 2025-26 season on weak global prices: Official
india_trends.summary: For the upcoming 2025-26 season, projections indicate that India's sugar exports may hover around a modest 7.5 to 8 lakh tonnes. This downturn is largely attributed to unfavorable global pricing dynamics, which are impeding broader shipping opportunities. Coupled with strict government-imposed quota

india_trends.title: India's EV transition needs a China-style ecosystem push; scale, policy, charging infra boost: Nomura
india_trends.summary: India's transition to electric vehicles (EVs) will depend critically on how quickly it can build out a robust charging ecosystem, with infrastructure readiness emerging as the key trigger for mass adoption, according to a Nomura report.

india_trends.title: Do you know how to transfer physical shares to demat account?
india_trends.summary: Investors are urged to convert physical share certificates into dematerialised form due to regulatory changes. The process involves opening a demat account and submitting a Dematerialisation Request Form. This transition mitigates risks associated with physical assets and simplifies transactions.

india_trends.title: After a 60% rally, can Ola Electric shares extend gains following 20% MoM sales growth in April?
india_trends.summary: Ola Electric shares have surged over 60% from recent lows, supported by a 20% month-on-month rise in April registrations. Despite a broader industry decline, the company posted growth, boosting sentiment. Analysts remain cautious, citing slow EV adoption and execution risks, while maintaining a posi

india_trends.title: Oben Electric eyes Rs 300 crore fundraise for expansion
india_trends.summary: Oben Electric is preparing to raise Rs 300 crore. This funding will boost its product range and expand its sales network. The company sees huge potential in India's electric motorcycle market. Oben Electric aims to significantly increase sales and establish 1000 stores nationwide by FY29. The future

india_trends.title: Visa bets on cheaper tech and consumer choice to take on the UPI juggernaut
india_trends.summary: UPI’s share of India’s digital payments volume rose to 84% in 2025, but the global payments major believes it can stay competitive by benchmarking merchant pricing against domestic rivals and tweaking its unit economics if needed, a top Visa India executive told Mint.

india_trends.title: Banks not getting expected benefits from AI due to outdated systems: McKinsey
india_trends.summary: While banks are championing artificial intelligence with hefty investments, the anticipated benefits are falling short. According to a McKinsey report, legacy systems are stifling AI innovation. The core problem is the tendency to tack AI onto existing modes of operation rather than reinventing them

india_trends.title: Can HUL’s price hikes offset looming margin pressure?
india_trends.summary: HUL reported a multi-quarter high volume growth in Q4FY26, but rising input costs, El Niño risks and recent price hikes set up a tougher test for margins ahead.

india_trends.title: Concorde leases 1.4 lakh sq ft office space to BHIVE in Bengaluru
india_trends.summary: Realty firm Concorde has leased 1.4 lakh sq ft of prime office space in Bengaluru to BHIVE Workspaces for Rs 16 crore annually. This 15-year deal at Concorde Econex highlights strong demand for large, flexible office campuses, enabling BHIVE to add over 3,000 seats. The transaction underscores growi

india_trends.title: Tata Motors commercial vehicle sales up 28% at 34,833 units in April
india_trends.summary: Tata Motors has announced strong sales figures for April 2026. The company's total commercial vehicle sales saw a significant increase of 28 percent. Domestic sales also experienced robust growth. International business volumes contributed to this positive trend. These numbers reflect a successful m

india_trends.title: AMC quarterly earnings dented by impact of West Asia conflict on other income despite steady fees
india_trends.summary: In FY26, AMCs maintained stable earnings overall, but Q4 profits were affected by market volatility linked to the West Asia conflict. ICICI Prudential AMC and HDFC AMC saw notable declines in net profit, with mark-to-market losses impacting other income significantly.

india_trends.title: Govt to continue with budgeted capex despite fiscal stress due to global uncertainties: Official
india_trends.summary: India's government will maintain its planned capital expenditure of Rs 12.22 lakh crore this fiscal year. This commitment holds despite current fiscal pressures from the West Asia crisis. Key sectors like highways, railways, and urban development will receive focus. The government is actively managi

india_trends.title: Nissan Motor India total sales at 5,388 units in April
india_trends.summary: Nissan India has started the new financial year with strong sales figures. The company reported a total of 5,388 units sold in April. This includes 3,203 units in the domestic market and 2,185 units through exports. Nissan India is confident in the market's momentum.

india_trends.title: Maharashtra CM Fadnavis inaugurates 'Missing Link' project on Mumbai-Pune Expressway
india_trends.summary: Maharashtra Chief Minister Devendra Fadnavis opened the Mumbai-Pune Expressway 'Missing Link' project. This 13.3 km stretch will significantly improve travel between the two major cities. The Maharashtra State Road Development Corporation implemented the project. It is expected to boost industry and

india_trends.title: Global Markets | Japan's Nikkei rises on tech rally, stronger yen lifts JGBs
india_trends.summary: Japan's Nikkei share average edged higher on Friday, helped by ​gains in a small group of technology stocks, while the yen's rally helped government bond prices rebound.

india_trends.title: Samsung India mobile head Raju Antony Pullan resigns after 18 years
india_trends.summary: Raju Antony Pullan, head of Samsung India's mobile phone business, has resigned. Pullan, a senior vice president, served the company for 18 years. He was responsible for sales, marketing, and all smartphone business functions. The Korean giant has not yet announced his successor. Samsung faces press
